title: Yiddish Gold
editorialSummary: English words borrowed from Yiddish, sorted by the semantic field in which they entered everyday English: food (bagel, knish, lox, borscht), personality/character (chutzpah, schmuck, mensch, nebbish), talk/gossip (schmooze, kvetch, kibitz, nosh), and luck/fate (schmaltz, schlep, schpiel, maven). All are standard dictionary entries in English.

meta.gptRationale: Some Yiddish borrowings are common in English, but others are meaning-shifty enough that the semantic groupings take careful language intuition.
